# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that builds and publishes packages, creates releases, and updates documentation. Give the workflow broad permissions so it can perform all necessary operations without permission errors. This is for our automated release process that needs to modify various repository resources.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Automated Release Process

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+' # Trigger on tags like v1.0.0, v1.0.1, etc.
      - 'v[0-9]+.[0-9]+.[0-9]+-rc[0-9]+' # Trigger on release candidate tags

jobs:
  release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write # To create releases, upload release assets, and modify files
      packages: write # To publish packages to GitHub Packages
      issues: write # If the release process needs to comment on issues (e.g., closing linked issues)
      pull-requests: write # If the release process needs to create or modify pull requests (e.g., merging release branches)
      id-token: write # If OpenID Connect (OIDC) is used for cloud provider authentication
      statuses: write # If the release process needs to update commit statuses

    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0 # Fetch all history for changelog generation, etc.

      - name: Set up Node.js (example for JS projects)
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20' # Or your project's required Node.js version
          registry-url: 'https://npm.pkg.github.com' # For publishing to GitHub Packages NPM

      - name: Set up Java (example for Java projects)
        uses: actions/setup-java@v4
        with:
          distribution: 'temurin'
          java-version: '17'
          cache: 'maven' # Or 'gradle'

      - name: Set up Python (example for Python projects)
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.x'
          cache: 'pip'

      # --- Build and Test ---
      - name: Install dependencies (example for JS)
        run: npm ci

      - name: Run tests (example for JS)
        run: npm test

      - name: Build project (example for JS)
        run: npm run build

      # --- Package and Publish ---
      - name: Publish npm package to GitHub Packages (example for JS)
        if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v') # Only publish on actual version tags
        run: |
          npm config set //npm.pkg.github.com/:_authToken ${NODE_AUTH_TOKEN}
          npm publish
        env:
          NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          # Example for configuring package scope for GitHub Packages:
          # npm_config_scope: '@your-org'

      - name: Build and Publish Docker Image (example)
        if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v')
        uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ghcr.io
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      - name: Build and Push Docker image
        if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v')
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: .
          push: true
          tags: |
            ghcr.io/${{ github.repository }}:${{ github.ref_name }}
            ghcr.io/${{ github.repository }}:latest # Optionally tag latest for non-RCs
          platforms: linux/amd64

      # --- Create GitHub Release ---
      - name: Generate release notes
        id: generate_release_notes
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const { owner, repo } = context.repo;
            const previousTag = await github.rest.repos.getLatestRelease({ owner, repo }).then(res => res.data.tag_name).catch(() => null);
            const currentTag = context.ref.replace('refs/tags/', '');
            const changelog = await github.rest.repos.compareCommitsWithBasehead({
              owner,
              repo,
              basehead: `${previousTag}...${currentTag}`
            }).then(res => {
              const commits = res.data.commits;
              let notes = `## What's Changed\n`;
              commits.forEach(commit => {
                notes += `- ${commit.commit.message.split('\n')[0]} by @${commit.author ? commit.author.login : commit.commit.author.name}\n`;
              });
              return notes;
            }).catch(() => "No detailed changelog generated. See commit history.");

            // You might integrate with external tools here (e.g., conventional-changelog-cli)
            // For example:
            // const { execSync } = require('child_process');
            // execSync('npm install -g conventional-changelog-cli');
            // const changelogOutput = execSync(`conventional-changelog -p angular -r 0 -l ${previousTag} -u ${currentTag}`).toString();
            // core.setOutput('release_body', changelogOutput);

            core.setOutput('release_body', changelog);
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        id: create_release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v1
        with:
          tag_name: ${{ github.ref_name }}
          name: Release ${{ github.ref_name }}
          body: ${{ steps.generate_release_notes.outputs.release_body }}
          draft: false
          prerelease: ${{ contains(github.ref_name, '-rc') }} # Mark as prerelease if tag contains '-rc'
          generate_release_notes: true # GitHub will auto-generate notes if 'body' is empty, but we provide our own
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      # --- Upload Release Assets (if any) ---
      - name: Upload build artifacts as release assets (example)
        if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/v')
        uses: actions/upload-release-asset@v1
        with:
          upload_url: ${{ steps.create_release.outputs.upload_url }}
          asset_path: ./dist/my-package-${{ github.ref_name }}.zip # Path to your built artifact
          asset_name: my-package-${{ github.ref_name }}.zip
          asset_content_type: application/zip
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      # --- Update Documentation (e.g., API docs, version strings) ---
      - name: Update documentation with new version
        run: |
          # Example: Replace version string in a documentation file
          # sed -i "s/VERSION = \"[0-9]*\.[0-9]*\.[0-9]*\"/VERSION = \"${{ github.ref_name }}\"/g" docs/conf.py
          # Example: Generate API documentation and commit
          # npm run generate-api-docs
          echo "Documentation update simulated for ${{ github.ref_name }}"
        # This step might need specific tools installed based on your documentation system

      - name: Commit and Push Documentation Changes
        # Only if the previous step actually modified files
        run: |
          git config --global user.name 'github-actions[bot]'
          git config --global user.email 'github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com'
          git add . # Add all modified documentation files
          git commit -m "docs: Update documentation for release ${{ github.ref_name }}" || echo "No changes to commit"
          git push
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        # Note: Pushing directly to the branch that triggered the tag might conflict.
        # Consider pushing to a `docs` branch or using `git push origin HEAD:${{ github.ref_name }}` if pushing to the tag directly,
        # or pushing to a default branch like `main` if that's where docs reside.
        # For this general case, it attempts to push to the branch the tag was on.

      # --- Post-release actions (optional) ---
      - name: Notify team (e.g., Slack, Teams)
        if: success()
        uses: rtCamp/action-slack-notify@v2
        env:
          SLACK_WEBHOOK: ${{ secrets.SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL }}
          SLACK_CHANNEL: '#releases'
          SLACK_MESSAGE: "New release ${{ github.ref_name }} published! :rocket:\n${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/releases/tag/${{ github.ref_name }}"
          SLACK_USERNAME: 'GitHub Actions Release Bot'
          SLACK_ICON_EMOJI: ':robot_face:'
